disclaimer: This directory contains public institutional resources only. No personal contact information is included. For connection to knowledge holders, elders, or tradition bearers, contact the relevant cultural center or organization listed here. They will facilitate appropriate introductions according to community protocols.

resources.name: Canadian Museum of History — First Peoples Hall
resources.description: National museum with extensive First Peoples permanent gallery covering thousands of years of Indigenous history, material culture, and contemporary expression. Includes significant Anishinaabe, Haudenosaunee, Cree, and Inuit collections.

resources.name: Inuit Tapiriit Kanatami (ITK) — Education Resources
resources.description: National Inuit organization representing four Inuit regions of Canada (Inuvialuit, Nunavut, Nunavik, Nunatsiavut). Primary source for Inuit-authorized educational content, research, and community programs. The IQ principles were developed in consultation with ITK.

resources.name: First Nations Information Governance Centre (FNIGC)
resources.description: The authoritative source for OCAP® principles and First Nations data governance frameworks. Provides resources for communities and organizations implementing OCAP® in research and educational contexts. Trademark holder for OCAP®.

resources.name: Aanischaaukamikw Cree Cultural Institute
resources.description: Cultural institute of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee in northern Quebec. Focuses on preserving and sharing Cree language, culture, and traditional knowledge. Programs include youth cultural education, elder knowledge transmission, and community archives.

resources.name: Ojibwe Cultural Foundation (OCF)
resources.description: Located on M'Chigeeng First Nation on Manitoulin Island, Ontario. Community cultural center focused on Anishinaabe language, art, and traditional knowledge preservation. Programs include traditional craft instruction, language classes, and cultural events.

resources.name: Nunavut Arctic College — Inuit Studies Program
resources.description: Post-secondary institution based in Iqaluit, Nunavut, offering programs in Inuit studies, Inuktitut language, traditional knowledge documentation, and northern livelihoods. A key institution for community-grounded Inuit education.

resources.name: Kanien'kehá:ka Onkwawén:na Raotitióhkwa Language and Cultural Center
resources.description: Cultural and language center located in Kahnawake Mohawk Territory, Quebec. Dedicated to the preservation and revitalization of Kanien'kéha (Mohawk language) and Haudenosaunee cultural practices. Offers language programs, cultural workshops, and community resources.

resources.name: Algonquins of Pikwakanagan First Nation — Cultural Programs
resources.description: The only Algonquin First Nation with a reserve in Ontario. Community programs include cultural heritage education, language preservation (Algonquin / Omàmiwininìmowin), and traditional skill workshops. Community-authorized point of contact for Algonquin cultural content.

resources.name: Musée des Abénakis
resources.description: Located in Odanak, Quebec, this museum is housed within the Abenaki community and focuses specifically on Abenaki history, culture, and material heritage. Includes extensive collections of traditional crafts, canoes, and community history. Community-operated and community-authorized.

resources.name: Musée Amérindien de Mashteuiatsh
resources.description: Located in Mashteuiatsh (Lac-Saint-Jean, Quebec), operated by the Pekuakamiulnuatsh (Lake Saint John Innu). Preserves and presents Innu (Ilnu) material culture, traditional knowledge, and community history. Community-operated and community-authorized.

resources.name: Dene Nation — Cultural Resources
resources.description: Political organization representing Dene peoples across the Northwest Territories. Provides information on Dene nations, communities, and cultural programs. Point of contact for resources related to Dene traditional knowledge and cultural programming.

resources.name: Indigenous Peoples Atlas of Canada
resources.description: A major collaborative publication by the Royal Canadian Geographical Society in partnership with First Nations, Inuit, and Métis organizations. Four-volume atlas covering Indigenous histories, territories, knowledge systems, and contemporary realities. Community-authorized content with nation-specific contributions.

resources.name: Odawa Native Friendship Centre — Programs
resources.description: Ottawa-based friendship centre serving urban Indigenous community members from across Canada. Provides community programs, cultural events, and connections to nation-specific cultural organizations. Useful gateway for learners seeking community connection in an urban context.

resources.name: Attikamek Cultural Organization (COSMA)
resources.description: Organization supporting Attikamek cultural programming across three Attikamek communities (Opitciwan, Wemotaci, Manawan) in the Mauricie region of Quebec. Programs include language revitalization and traditional knowledge documentation.

resources.name: Nunavik Regional Board of Health and Social Services — Cultural Resources
resources.description: Regional board serving the Nunavik Inuit of northern Quebec. Provides health and social services that integrate Inuit cultural knowledge and IQ principles. Educational resources for Nunavik Inuit cultural practices are available through their programs and publications.
